title: Doc Strings
description: Human-written prose keyed by command/attribute/constant/directive/control-keyword name (or a ui.* key for interface chrome). Powers Monaco hover docs (Phase 1.6) and the ALT-hover popup system (Phase 3.5) — CREATION_PLAN.md 3.5 says to reuse the same hover content for both, which is why this is one shared file rather than two. Community-maintained by PR; the Definitive RMS Scripting Guide (Zetnus) is the seed source pending permission to adapt its prose — see PLAN.md open questions. Write original summaries here, don't copy the guide's text verbatim.

key.description: Matches a name in language.json (a command/attribute/directive/controlKeyword) or game-constants.json (an rmsConstant), or a "ui." prefixed key for ALT-hover on non-code interface elements (Phase 3.5), e.g. "ui.mapPreview.currentFinalToggle".

summary.description: One sentence. Shown in the Monaco hover tooltip and as the ALT-hover popup's headline.

details.description: Optional longer prose for the ALT-hover popup — examples, gotchas, defaults worth calling out.

seeAlso.description: Other entry keys worth cross-referencing, e.g. land_percent's entry might list number_of_tiles.

entries.summary: Creates one land mass, sized and positioned by its attributes.
entries.details: The most common way to place non-player territory (islands, center land, etc). For player starting areas, use create_player_lands instead, which takes the same attributes but distributes evenly across all players.

entries.summary: Sets this land's size as a percentage of the total map area.
entries.details: Mutually exclusive with number_of_tiles — use whichever is more natural for the effect you want; percent scales with map size, an absolute tile count doesn't.
